mutations.name: createCommitOnBranch
mutations.description:
Appends a commit to the given branch as the authenticated user.

This mutation creates a commit whose parent is the HEAD of the provided branch and also updates that branch to point to the new commit. It can be thought of as similar to git commit.

Locating a Branch

Commits are appended to a branch of type Ref. This must refer to a git branch (i.e. the fully qualified path must begin with refs/heads/, although including this prefix is optional.

Callers may specify the branch to commit to either by its global node ID or by passing both of repositoryNameWithOwner and refName. For more details see the documentation for CommittableBranch.

Describing Changes

fileChanges are specified as a FilesChanges object describing FileAdditions and FileDeletions.

Please see the documentation for FileChanges for more information on how to use this argument to describe any set of file changes.

Authorship

Similar to the web commit interface, this mutation does not support specifying the author or committer of the commit and will not add support for this in the future.

A commit created by a successful execution of this mutation will be authored by the owner of the credential which authenticates the API request. The committer will be identical to that of commits authored using the web interface.

If you need full control over author and committer information, please use the Git Database REST API instead.

Commit Signing

Commits made using this mutation are automatically signed by GitHub if supported and will be marked as verified in the user interface.

returnFields.name: clientMutationId
returnFields.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation.

returnFields.name: ref
returnFields.description: The ref which has been updated to point to the new commit.

fields.name: abbreviatedOid
fields.description: An abbreviated version of the Git object ID.

fields.name: additions
fields.description: The number of additions in this commit.

fields.name: associatedPullRequests
fields.description: The merged Pull Request that introduced the commit to the repository. If the commit is not present in the default branch, additionally returns open Pull Requests associated with the commit.

fields.name: changedFiles
fields.description: We recommend using the changedFilesIfAvailable field instead of changedFiles, as changedFiles will cause your request to return an error if GitHub is unable to calculate the number of changed files.
fields.deprecationReason: changedFiles will be removed. Use changedFilesIfAvailable instead. Removal on 2023-01-01 UTC.

fields.name: changedFilesIfAvailable
fields.description: The number of changed files in this commit. If GitHub is unable to calculate the number of changed files (for example due to a timeout), this will return null. We recommend using this field instead of changedFiles.

fields.name: checkSuites
fields.description: The check suites associated with a commit.

arguments.description: Allows specifying a beginning time or date for fetching commits. Unexpected results may be returned for dates not between 1970-01-01 and 2099-12-13 (inclusive).

arguments.name: until
arguments.description: Allows specifying an ending time or date for fetching commits. Unexpected results may be returned for dates not between 1970-01-01 and 2099-12-13 (inclusive).

fields.name: minimizedReason
fields.description: Returns why the comment was minimized. One of abuse, off-topic, outdated, resolved, duplicate, spam, and low-quality. Note that the case and formatting of these values differs from the inputs to the MinimizeComment mutation.

enums.name: CommitContributionOrderField
enums.description: Properties by which commit contribution connections can be ordered.

values.name: COMMIT_COUNT
values.description: Order commit contributions by how many commits they represent.

values.name: OCCURRED_AT
values.description: Order commit contributions by when they were made.

enums.name: ComparisonStatus
enums.description: The status of a git comparison between two refs.

values.name: AHEAD
values.description: The head ref is ahead of the base ref.

values.name: BEHIND
values.description: The head ref is behind the base ref.

values.name: DIVERGED
values.description: The head ref is both ahead and behind of the base ref, indicating git history has diverged.

values.name: IDENTICAL
values.description: The head ref and base ref are identical.

unions.name: StatusCheckRollupContext

inputObjects.name: CommitAuthor
inputObjects.description: Specifies an author for filtering Git commits.

inputFields.name: emails
inputFields.description: Email addresses to filter by. Commits authored by any of the specified email addresses will be returned.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: ID of a User to filter by. If non-null, only commits authored by this user will be returned. This field takes precedence over emails.
